Incarnadine Breastplate  
 

Lore Item
Slot: CHEST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 35
STR: +15 DEX: +15 INT: +5 HP: +25
Effect: Gather Shadows (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 8.0) at Level 50
WT: 7.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR SHD
Race: ALL except IKS
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Incarnadine Breastplate
Lore Item
Slot: CHEST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 35
STR: +15 DEX: +15 INT: +5 HP: +25
Effect: Gather Shadows (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 8.0) at Level 50
WT: 7.0 Size: MEDIUM

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Plate
Tint:

Stackable:No
Merchant Value:30 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:17069

Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-20 11:04:55
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 4,500pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Chardok
NPC Name
Overking Bathezid


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Chardok

« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
Through the Door Trick?
QuoteReply
# Jan 05 2012 at 1:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592790337356
Scholar
63 posts
I've been trying to get this chest piece for my warrior and have always used the "remove illusion to get through doors" trick and I've recently found that my gnome-box, can't do this trick in Chardok. I could get into the Prince room and the locked door that leads down to the Overlord with my OGRE warrior, but my GNOME couldnt. I tried Spectre/skleteon/frost goblin, illusions and no luck.

Also, tried the camp out against the door, log back in method and no luck there either.
▲ Page top
Through the Door Trick?
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2012 at 1:29 PM Rating: Decent
Sevlore
Scholar
41 posts
I've found that the illusion trick works best when going from a smaller illusion to a bigger race, hence it working no problem for ogre. Not sure what you can do about it for gnome. Keep trying the frost goblin illusion, I guess, since it is small like a gnome. It may work eventually.

My brother plays a gnome shadow knight and he has to use his shadow step aa to get through doors. When I play my halfling druid I use a mount, though I don't guess you can use mounts in Chardok.

Sorry this wasn't more helpful.

Good luck!
____________________________
Sevlore Fisti`cuffs
100 Ashenhand
<Eye of Eternity>
Erolisi Marr
▲ Page top
bah
QuoteReply
# Jul 12 2008 at 10:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592778485602
54 posts
Iksar and Ogres are the best races in the game, superior to Drakkin. The only talent Drakkin have is a low DPS, bugged AE cone which can be used once every 5 minutes - and when I say low DPS, I mean seriously low. It will do maybe 3-5% dmg on a lvl 75 TSS trash mob.

Iksar have an AC bonus which is vastly superior to that stupid breath weapon, and Ogres cannot be stunned from the front. Both of those innates put the drakkin BW to shame.

I agree that Iksar should be able to wear this BP; but Drakkin are in no way a better race than Iksar
▲ Page top
out of the new races why still no iskar
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2007 at 10:02 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591912082307
23 posts
Not to speak to ill of SOE but how is it after all these expansions and races added to norrath that soe STILL prohibits iskars from using this item im mean seriously even freaking drakkins could use it soe needs to add iskar to the list its kindof BS there left out when even a special race like drakkin which has its own special talents and was essentially drakkin specific armor geared would have abilities to wear such old expansion equipment but not iskars?
▲ Page top
out of the new races why still no iskar
QuoteReply
# Sep 17 2007 at 12:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592261987632
55 posts
it will never be iksar usable, due to the rules that came out. all new armor will most likely have iksar, but due to this item is from kunark content, they arent gonna rechange kunark content.
▲ Page top
out of the new races why still no iskar
QuoteReply
# Aug 11 2007 at 5:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1600647551928
Scholar
1,352 posts
when Kunark was released, Iksar could not wear any plate armor from the exspansion. The reasoning was because iksars have an innate AC bonus. However once the following exspansion came out they began to itemize iksar into the plate armor sets.
▲ Page top

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 19 2005 at 8:48 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is why I get frustrated with this site. Who the hell wants to sit and read down 40 posts that tell you pretty much nothing. I dont care how it looks, I dont care how much it costs. I like to know the stats and where the guy is located. Why cant you folks put in the map locations as I do when I find a spawn that isnt on the ingame map? Put useful information on here. I understand the occasional rage about something, I do that myself but how it looks is not necessary info. Is this EQ or GQ? Also cant you administrators put the spawn location somewhere in your info as well? Would help.
RE: Where does he spawn?
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2005 at 6:40 PM Rating: Decent
dafdaf
View User Forum
Scholar
1,273 posts
I agree that sometimes it's nice to go to the page and just see the information you are looking for, but it's also nice to find extra information that you aren't. I love going alla-surfing from page to page following the links and info that I didn't expect to find.

But as a rule of thumb you go to the item page to hear it's price, uses, how it looks etc, and you go to the page about the mob to find it's loc, hp, hits etc.

Edited, Sun Sep 4 19:46:12 2005
▲ Page top
Rarity?
QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2004 at 1:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592267279119
Scholar
39 posts
The item can't be that rare, one sells for 60k in bazaar and I bought two last night for 40k each on Luclin...my family has one lvl 35 warrior and two SK's below 30. As new players to EQ (week before Holloween) who will probably never see a RAID with a Guild to get this BP, it was money well spent IMO. Thanks for the info on when the spell effect will function. I expect this piece of armor will be worn for quite a long time by our characters...
My warrior was blessed on Holloween with a No-Drop Steel Rod of the Knight which as made a huge difference, now add this BP, and I might live longer...
One reason for the sudden popularity might be players giving up their gear to leave EQ, I have been the lucky choosen one for several players passing on their Uber gear before departing the game. Others are selling off stuff passed to them, for the cash.

# Jan 13 2006 at 3:07 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591863751171
877 posts
The poster's name is "Lady SilentRedd". Hate to sound sexist, but, when it comes to player interactions in EQ, females get a sickening number of handouts. My girlfriend and I play together all of the time. When we go to donate for buffs, we pay the same amount, but they generally don't accept her plat, while they've always taken mine, without fail. People keep offering her items, guilds, power levels, "advice", etc. All I get are beggars.. which is pretty sad, since I'm new to the server.

Edited, Fri Jan 13 03:08:34 2006

Got this last night on the first kill of the King for a Cleric epic. Made one of our guild warriors very happy, even if the AC isn't high enough for tanking it'll be useful for travelling to and from camp spots.
▲ Page top
Boy, I must be lucky
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2003 at 8:14 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247179407
View User Forum
Scholar
155 posts
Went down to the nobles today as our guild's 2nd trip there. First one we had pets aggro and setoff traps and ended up with 33 coffins usesd but we learned.

So this time We go down with 26 of us and make it no problem. Took Prince, then moved to King. Killed him twice and each time he dropped this BP.

Currently using the Indicolite from Hate and this is very similar in basic stats. Both are AC35 STR15 DEX15 but this one has 5INT and 25HP whereas the Indicolite has 15STA. The biggest difference is the Effect. Too Cool!

Kharlkuhlinayn
Morell Thule

got this little gem last night on my 61 halfling warrior. This is going to save me the 120 plat a day i was spending on invis potions =). The king was a wuss, but you better burn down the queen fast as you can.
▲ Page top
Sweet BP, pretty rare
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2002 at 2:48 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591773300270
5 posts
I got this after 4 weekends spent killing (or trying kill) the queen for the warrior epic. Ill tell yea thats one mean *****. We had 6 full groups all over 52nd with 50% of the crew at 60th level. And with all that firepower we only got her 4 times. The king was a cream puff compaired to the queen.
We killed the King around 12 times total over 6 or 7 raids(one raid we got him 4 times). Only one BP droped. Seems pretty rare drop based on this small sample set of data. Good luck finding one.
-Your pusherman
▲ Page top
RE: Sweet BP, pretty rare
QuoteReply
# Dec 17 2002 at 9:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595175379145
12 posts
My guild did Queen 2 times that I know of in one day, the King dropped 2 BPs both times we killed him, Queen only gave us the Blade 2 times and Shard of Night once (I think 1 time wasn't there for the 2nd kill). Only thing that got some of my guild was the AoE, they were the lvl 50 and some odd ppl who fell to it. Everyone else was in there at least mid 50s to early 60s.

# Sep 22 2002 at 9:40 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591756030838

Queen falls pretty easy. Her AoE appears to be directional. Just have to avoid it and pile on the damage.

First time our guild killed everyone was saying "Is that all there is?". Killed her twice, 1 death total.

QuoteReply
# Mar 31 2002 at 10:47 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591769899116
Scholar
19 posts
Proc at level 50
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 04 2002 at 7:57 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) It doesnt proc its triggered...just right click it at any level
RE: Effect Level
QuoteReply
# Jun 09 2002 at 5:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779037916
38 posts
proc is the term used for "when" it can be triggered left clicking at level 1-49 will result in you wasting your time clicking it. no matter how many times you click it you wont get the effect. now i bet you wish clicking gave exp then youd click and click until youre fifty then itd happen and youd turn invis. ah well dont sprain your hand clicking it clicky clicky palladius click gather click
▲ Page top
RE: Effect Level
QuoteReply
# Jun 29 2002 at 12:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758247381
10 posts
I may be mistaken but, I do believe that a 'triggered' effect is one that a player enables by right-clicking on an item (such as 'Reviviscence' from the cleric epic weapon) whereas a 'proc' is a random occurence depending on one's dexterity (such as 'Siphon' from the Blood Point)...

Regards,


Laiderone Apoildru
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 14 2002 at 10:18 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) think that was covered about 5 posts ago
RE: Effect Level
QuoteReply
# Sep 16 2002 at 1:00 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595780131429
464 posts
So let me get this straight...

Do you have to click this to get the effect to work or will you just randomly turn invisible for fractions of seconds while you are fighting?

SNOOGINS!

(FYI - I was being a smartass)
▲ Page top
Phat Lewt
QuoteReply
# Nov 28 2001 at 10:33 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1594570593384
2 posts
I got one of these during guild raid this past weekend. I would take this over Indi bp any day, because of the invis, any spells to help a warrior. As far as the Queen goes, best way to kill her is need 60 warrior to engage her while chanters rune and chain heal warrior. Her AE spell only effects those in her eyesight, so getting behind her is the key. Place the 60 warrior up front, all other melees classes behind her and NO ONE TAUNT besides 60 warrior. Also helps to have fire pets to take the AE blast also. All other classes must stay in the pit in the King room against wall. The key is to get her back to the pit that way all non-melee classes are safe. It is tough, but very possible.....remember 60 warrior, fire pets and you have to believe!

53rd Season of Meatshield training
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 15 2003 at 9:18 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) chain heal against queen? heh. maybe back in 2001.
Nice BP
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 9:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361120086
8 posts
Traded a Venomic Axe, Fungi Tunic and the Edge of the Taskmaster for this today. With the insanely slow or no Drop Indo BP in hate and the revampeed dok being a bit tougher this will carry me thru to 58 or higher as I will always keep this BP for the effect in my sack. Effect and stats are very nice combo. GL all

# Nov 01 2001 at 4:48 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767386144
9 posts
Ok, doing the queen with like 4 tanks is way gay to post here, have you been hunting sarnaks in LoIO, better put your post in another section man, because everyone knows that her AoE will fart you to death before you even blink.

gees man ppl come here to get useful information and when people post crap like that just makes the forum plain gay.

the right way to get her is by killing her in like 30-40 seconds or the raid is roasted, please stop putting NooB info on these boards unless you really know what the hell you are talking about, you migh even make some people think they could do a queen raid with 4 tanks, maybe you like to see peoples doing CR in Chardok, really fun when you get in deep for sure /bleh.

/bonk Bruce Lee


Mohan - 58 Master on Rodcet Nife

I don't see why not.. 4 warriors? Hell.. there's potions for calling mage pets, 300hp insta heals that work as fast as you can click, damage shields.. You would, erm.. of course be able to buy 4 incarnadine bp's about 6 times over for the cost though ;)

RE: man gotta love noobs
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2001 at 8:15 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591769051269
74 posts
Well you added a lot of useful information. Anyway, I think if you were to have thought a little deeper into what he was writing, im sure you'd realize that he didnt mean, a group of 4 tanks went in to kill the queen. Stop and think before you flame someone. Not everyone rereads there posts and uses spell checkers and grammer checkers so as to make them selves look smarter and therefor the better person. Ive heard stories of like 2 groups killing the queen so there is more than one way of doing it. If your way works fine do it that way, if someone else's way works, well hell at least she's dead. The hatred that spews from people over the smallest things is crazy /shiver

# Dec 17 2002 at 9:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595175379145
12 posts
When my guild did this we did it real quick, had only 3 warriors and neither of us for down to 75% health during the fight. We didn't fight it in the room, we pulled her and the King out into the hallway and fought her there. Only had the ppl in their low 50s die to the AoE and thats about it.
▲ Page top
BP
QuoteReply
# Aug 30 2001 at 12:43 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
As an SK, all I can say is, "too bad it has GS effect". MY guild or any intelligent raiding guild that would beat down the Overking is going to most likely award this to a Warrior because it will simply benefit the guild more on raids to have a warrior who can invis. Some silly melee who already has an Invis spell will not get this BP unless the guild is pretty foolish. Even a warrior who had a better BP would take it for the invis, stuff it in a wt reduction bag and carry it everywhere. I'm sure there are a few SK's running around with this, but they were really lucky if all they had to do was win a roll and walk off with it.

▲ Page top
RE: BP
QuoteReply
# Dec 23 2001 at 10:58 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762086122
235 posts
▲ Page top
RE: BP
QuoteReply
# Dec 23 2001 at 11:04 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591762086122
235 posts
sorry bout my last, blank, post hehe...
There are many ways to invis a warrior. Aside from the fact that no warrior at this level should ever be alone and thus without an inviser, there are invis potions (not too expensive either if i remember correct, not dirt cheap but not unreasonable), and other, easier to get items whos value would not be so wasted in having it for the porposes of the effect. Both SKs and warriors would benefit from this greatly. Even though SKs can invis themselves, we have better things to do with our spell slots and so having it as an item effect means we can mem more usful things (thats the only thing that makes BE good, we can do all our stuff instead of just 7 things [gotta keep 1 slot for emergency FD in most situations hehe])
▲ Page top

# Dec 17 2002 at 9:24 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595175379145
12 posts
Everyone in my guild already had better but one warrior, but he wasn't there to get dibs on it, so we used our raid point system for it and I ended up winning it. The high SK in my guild passed up on it for the same reason that was posted above, he had the spell and the circlet already even though he only had a Barbed Ringmail for his BP. Very classy of him, I would have passed it up to him if he wanted, I can always buy invis potions to make up for it.

# Jul 27 2001 at 9:42 AM Rating: Decent

Chardok ain't that hard really, as long as you don't ***** the pulls up. We took down the queen with only 4 55+ tanks (Was scary), but if you have someone who is a bad puller you are all doomed =P. This i a nice BP though, and now since they nerfed teh CoS this BP is even better! I am hoping to throw one on my SK soon.

Make sure to tell them the whole story. You were supported by...enchanter, healers (note the plural), and maybe a hybrid.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 02 2001 at 11:56 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Heh, I'm only posting this here because this is probably the highest profile non-quest item in the zone, so someone may actually see it.
An Alternative...
QuoteReply
# May 18 2001 at 2:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591753354195
Scholar
80 posts
For you ShadowKights that have a better breastplate, but like the effect on this one, take some friends to Droga and get a Circlet of Shadows. Same effect, only it has no cast time (can use even while running, or even recast in front of a mob and he won't even see you casting). In one evening you can get 2 or 3 of them.

Note: The circlet doesn't have to be worn to use the effect.
▲ Page top
RE: An Alternative...
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2001 at 12:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762841996
3 posts
If you get the circlet, buy a pre-nerf, Patch not too long ago gave the circlets a casting time, all circlets currently in circulation were not affected
▲ Page top
RE: An Alternative...
QuoteReply
# Jun 11 2001 at 3:47 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757502218
23 posts
I currently have the Circlet(EXCELLENT ITEM). I must agree, unless you have a better BP this is the one for SK's. Being a Dark Elf as I am, I really like the Str bonus on this. I will be looking forward to Chardok. Maybe I can get me another Earring of Station here too :)
▲ Page top
RE: An Alternative...
QuoteReply
# May 24 2001 at 5:54 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Yes, Circlet of Shadows is awesome. 0 cast time, and can be put in inventory slot and just right clicked/hotbuttoned. Great for exploring places that don't see invis, such as... droga, where the cos drops. IMO, this bp would go best for a warrior, since they are a class that are not supposed to have invisibility, this would be a great asset for the effect, but an awesome bp for either class.
▲ Page top
Why I like this BP
QuoteReply
# May 03 2001 at 3:20 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This is a fantastic BP! On a Barb warrior it is blood red in color.

I lose a few HP (one or two hit's worth) when I use this in place of Indicolite (due to the loss of the +15sta).

The benefit of invis is...well...transparent! (could not help myself, sorry) I can now run through Kael (WL to EW), to the safe hall in Chardok (assuming all the dogs are dead or wandered off), getting to Phinny, <insert ANYwhere that you use invis> without the help of an Inviser. Also, while banking breaks the invis, using Bind Wound does not (also very handy). I keep finding situations where invis is either helpful, or down right lifesaving.

Only two reasons I can think of that a warrior would opt for Indicolite over Incarnadine:

1) Their stamina is less than 100 without the Indicolite BP.

2) They feel that Indicolite looks better
▲ Page top
RE: Why I like this BP
QuoteReply
# Dec 17 2002 at 9:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595175379145
12 posts
This is my opinion on looks, this is not a flame, if your looking for armor for LOOKS instead of stats and such then become a fashion model. I am 57 and I am a rainbow warrior, I wear Silver, Dark Blue, Green, and what my guildies call a Pink helm (its really red they just don't know it). I don't really care about looks, this is just a personal opinion of mine. As long as I get the stats I need I will wear Pink and Neon Green for crying out loud.
▲ Page top
Information
QuoteReply
# Apr 27 2001 at 10:30 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591750631095
2 posts
The effect has a casting time of roughly 8 seconds (I could be off), is a right click effect, and produces an invis effect. On a barbarian warrior, the BP is a deep red, and looks pretty cool in contrast to the browns of Indicolite, and the blacks of cobalt boots and bracers. Chardok is an insanely tough zone. Expect a full (30) crew of people 55 and over... and even then you might have it rough.

Kalran
53rd Warrior of Morell-Thule
<Triune>
